text:

  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is a mural.",
      "It is a true fresco."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has ten robust steel-framed panels bolted together.",
      "It has a total weight of about 23 short tons.",
      "It has a size of 22 ft by 74 ft."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by its deliberate design for portability.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Frida Kahlo.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Diego Rivera.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Paulette Goddard.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Timothy L. Pflueger.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Adolf Hitler.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Benito Mussolini.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Coatlicue, Aztec Goddess of Life.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Dudley C. Carter.",
      "It is characterized by its depiction of Sarah Gerstel."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for displaying the artistic expression of the North and South of the Americas.",
      "It is used for symbolizing the union between north and south.",
      "It is used for representing the creative genius of the South.",
      "It is used for showing elements from past and present.",
      "It is used for depicting the plastification of creative power of the Northern Mechanism.",
      "It is used for illustrating trends of creative effort in the United States.",
      "It is used for showing the rise of woman in various fields of creative endeavor.",
      "It is used for representing the creative culture of North developing from the necessity of making life possible in a new and empty land.",
      "It is used for complementing the exhibition Diego Rivera's America."
    ],

      "It is located at the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art.",
      "It is located on the City College of San Francisco campus."

    ],

    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "It is part of the Art in Action exhibition.",
      "It is part of the Golden Gate International Exposition.",
      "It is part of the Diego Rivera Theater lobby."
    ],
    "MemberOf": [

    "BelongsTo": [
      "It belongs to City College of San Francisco."
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "It is known for being the centerpiece of the Art In Action exhibit.",
      "It is known for its large size, being Rivera's largest contiguous work.",
      "It is known for its theme of Pan-American unity.",
      "It is known for its depiction of the marriage of Mexican artistry and US technology.",
      "It is known for the controversy over its caricatures of Hitler and Mussolini."
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "It is associated with the Art in Action exhibition.",
      "It is associated with the Golden Gate International Exposition.",
      "It is associated with Treasure Island.",

      "It occurred in 1940.",
      "It occurred in 1941.",

      "It occurred in 1961.",
      "It occurred in 1995.",
      "It occurred in 1999.",
      "It occurred in 2012.",
      "It occurred from June 2021 to 2024.",
      "It occurred as of January 2026."

      "unknown"

    "About": [
      "It is about the marriage of the artistic expression of the North and of the South on this Continent.",
      "It is about the blending of the art of the Indian, the Mexican, the Eskimo, with the urge that makes the machine.",
      "It is about American art being the result of a conjunction between the creative mechanism of the North and the creative power of the South.",
      "It is about the inspiration artists in North America should take from New World native arts.",
      "It is about the multicultural artistic expression forming into a unified cultural entity.",
      "It is about the eventual unity of the Americas.",
      "It is about the creative genius of the South growing from religious fervor and a native talent for plastic expression.",
      "It is about elements from past and present.",
      "It is about the plastification of creative power of the Northern Mechanism by union with the plastic tradition of the South.",
      "It is about trends of creative effort in the United States and the rise of woman in various fields of creative endeavor through her use of the power of manmade machinery."
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "It was influenced by the Art in Action exhibition.",
      "It was influenced by the Golden Gate International Exposition.",
      "It was influenced by the destruction of Man at the Crossroads.",
      "It was influenced by Europe plunging back into war.",
      "It was influenced by Roland J. McKinney's statement.",
      "It was influenced by traditional deep-rooted Southern Indian forms.",
      "It was influenced by New World native arts.",
      "It was influenced by Mexican artistry.",
      "It was influenced by US technology.",
      "It was influenced by Rivera's firmly held belief that multicultural artistic expression will form into a unified cultural entity."
    ],
    "Influenced": [
      "It influenced the design of the CCSF campus theater lobby.",
      "It influenced the construction of a future Diego Rivera Performing Arts Center."
    ]

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それは壁画です。",
      "それは真のフレスコ画です。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは10枚の頑丈な鋼鉄フレームパネルでボルトで固定されています。",
      "それは合計で約23ショートトン（21トン）の重さがあります。",
      "それは22フィート x 74フィート（6.7メートル x 22.6メートル）のサイズです。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは持ち運び可能であるように意図的に設計されているという特徴があります。",
      "それはフリーダ・カーロの描写が特徴です。",
      "それはディエゴ・リベラの描写が特徴です。",
      "それはポーレット・ゴダールの描写が特徴です。",
      "それはティモシー・L・プルイガーの描写が特徴です。",
      "それはアドルフ・ヒトラーの風刺画が特徴です。",
      "それはベニート・ムッソリーニの風刺画が特徴です。",
      "それは生命の女神コアトリクーの描写が特徴です。",
      "それはダドリー・C・カーターの描写が特徴です。",
      "それはサラ・ガーシュテルの描写が特徴です。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは南北アメリカの芸術的表現を表示するために使用されます。",
      "それは南北間の統合を象徴するために使用されます。",
      "それは南部の創造的才能の成長を示すために使用されます。",
      "それは過去と現在の要素を示すために使用されます。",
      "それは北部の創造力のプラスチック化を示すために使用されます。",
      "それはアメリカ合衆国における創造的努力の傾向を示すために使用されます。",
      "それは女性の創造的分野での台頭を示すために使用されます。",
      "それは新しい空の土地で生活を可能にする必要性から発展する北部の創造的文化を表すために使用されます。",
      "それはディエゴ・リベラのアメリカ展を補完するために使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それはサンフランシスコ近代美術館にあります。",
      "それはサンフランシスコ・シティ・カレッジのキャンパスにあります。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "それはサンフランシスコで建設されました。"
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "それはメキシコの芸術家で壁画家であるディエゴ・リベラによって建設されました。"
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "それはカリフォルニア州サンフランシスコで生まれました。"
    ],

      "unknown"
    ],

    ],

    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "それは5つのセクションに配置された10枚のパネルで構成されています。",
      "それは各セクションに大きな上部パネルと小さな下部パネルで構成されています。"
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "それはアート・イン・アクション展の一部です。",
      "それはゴールデンゲート国際博覧会の一部です。",
      "それはカレッジのディエゴ・リベラ・シアターのロビーの一部です。"
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "それはサンフランシスコ・シティ・カレッジに属しています。"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "それはアート・イン・アクション展の中心作品として知られています。",
      "それはリベラの最大の連続作品であるというその大きなサイズで知られています。",
      "それはパンアメリカン・ユニティというテーマで知られています。",
      "それはメキシコ芸術と米国技術の結婚の描写で知られています。",
      "それはヒトラーとムッソリーニの風刺画をめぐる論争で知られています。"
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "それはアート・イン・アクション展と関連があります。",
      "それはゴールデンゲート国際博覧会と関連があります。",
      "それはトレジャーアイランドと関連があります。",
      "それはサンフランシスコ・ジュニア・カレッジと関連があります。",
      "それはサンフランシスコ・シティ・カレッジと関連があります。",
      "それはティモシー・プルイガーと関連があります。",
      "それはエミー・ルー・パッカードと関連があります。",
      "それはモナ・ホフマンと関連があります。",
      "それはセルマ・ジョンソン・ストリートと関連があります。",
      "それはアーサー・ニエンドルフと関連があります。"
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "それはカリフォルニア州サンフランシスコで発生しました。",
      "それは1940年に発生しました。",
      "それは1941年に発生しました。",
      "それは1942年に発生しました。",
      "それは1961年に発生しました。",
      "それは1995年に発生しました。",
      "それは1999年に発生しました。",
      "それは2012年に発生しました。",
      "それは2021年6月から2024年まで発生しました。",
      "それは2026年1月現在発生しています。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "それはトレジャーアイランドのゴールデンゲート国際博覧会で展示されました。",
      "それはアート・イン・アクション展で展示されました。",
      "それはサンフランシスコ近代美術館のロバーツ・ファミリー・ギャラリーで展示されました。"
    ],
    "WonBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "About": [
      "それはこの大陸における北と南の芸術的表現の結婚についてです。",
      "それはインディアン、メキシコ人、エスキモーの芸術と機械を作るという衝動の融合についてです。",
      "それはアメリカ美術が北部の創造的メカニズムと南部の創造的力の結合の結果であることについてです。",
      "それは北米の芸術家が新世界先住民芸術から得るべきインスピレーションについてです。",
      "それは多文化芸術的表現が統一された文化的実体へと形成されることについてです。",
      "それはアメリカの最終的な統一についてです。",
      "それは宗教的熱意と塑性表現のネイティブな才能から成長する南部の創造的才能についてです。",
      "それは過去と現在の要素についてです。",
      "それは南部の塑性伝統との統合による北部の創造力のプラスチック化についてです。",
      "それはアメリカ合衆国における創造的努力の傾向と、男性が作った機械の力による様々な分野での女性の台頭についてです。"
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "それはアート・イン・アクション展の影響を受けました。",
      "それはゴールデンゲート国際博覧会の影響を受けました。",
      "それは「マン・アット・ザ・クロスロード」の破壊の影響を受けました。",
      "それはヨーロッパが再び戦争に突入した影響を受けました。",
      "それはローランド・J・マッキニーの発言の影響を受けました。",
      "それは伝統的な根深い南部のインディアンの形態の影響を受けました。",
      "それは新世界先住民芸術の影響を受けました。",
      "それはメキシコ芸術の影響を受けました。",
      "それは米国技術の影響を受けました。",
      "それはリベラの、多文化芸術的表現が統一された文化的実体を形成するという確固たる信念の影響を受けました。"
    ],
    "Influenced": [
      "それはCCSFキャンパスシアターロビーのデザインに影響を与えました。",
      "それは将来のディエゴ・リベラ舞台芸術センターの建設に影響を与えました。"
    ]
  }
}

HasA: It has ten robust steel-framed panels bolted together.
It has a total weight of about 23 short tons.
It has a size of 22 ft by 74 ft.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its deliberate design for portability.
It is characterized by its depiction of Frida Kahlo.
It is characterized by its depiction of Diego Rivera.
It is characterized by its depiction of Paulette Goddard.
It is characterized by its depiction of Timothy L. Pflueger.
It is characterized by its depiction of Adolf Hitler.
It is characterized by its depiction of Benito Mussolini.
It is characterized by its depiction of Coatlicue, Aztec Goddess of Life.
It is characterized by its depiction of Dudley C. Carter.
It is characterized by its depiction of Sarah Gerstel.
UsedFor: It is used for displaying the artistic expression of the North and South of the Americas.
It is used for symbolizing the union between north and south.
It is used for representing the creative genius of the South.
It is used for showing elements from past and present.
It is used for depicting the plastification of creative power of the Northern Mechanism.
It is used for illustrating trends of creative effort in the United States.
It is used for showing the rise of woman in various fields of creative endeavor.
It is used for representing the creative culture of North developing from the necessity of making life possible in a new and empty land.
It is used for complementing the exhibition Diego Rivera's America.
LocatedIn: It is located at the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art.
It is located on the City College of San Francisco campus.
BuiltIn: It was built in San Francisco.
BuiltBy: It was built by Mexican artist and muralist Diego Rivera.
OriginatedIn: It originated in San Francisco, California.
CreatedBy: It was created by Diego Rivera.
CreatedIn: It was created in San Francisco.
PublishedIn: It was published in Life magazine.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Diego Rivera.
ConsistsOf: It consists of ten panels arranged in five sections.
It consists of a larger upper panel and a smaller lower panel in each section.
PartOf: It is part of the Art in Action exhibition.
It is part of the Golden Gate International Exposition.
It is part of the Diego Rivera Theater lobby.
BelongsTo: It belongs to City College of San Francisco.
KnownFor: It is known for being the centerpiece of the Art In Action exhibit.
It is known for its large size, being Rivera's largest contiguous work.
It is known for its theme of Pan-American unity.
It is known for its depiction of the marriage of Mexican artistry and US technology.
It is known for the controversy over its caricatures of Hitler and Mussolini.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Art in Action exhibition.
It is associated with the Golden Gate International Exposition.
It is associated with Treasure Island.
It is associated with San Francisco Junior College.
It is associated with City College of San Francisco.
It is associated with Timothy Pflueger.
It is associated with Emmy Lou Packard.
It is associated with Mona Hoffman.
It is associated with Thelma Johnson Streat.
It is associated with Arthur Niendorff.
OccurredIn: It occurred in San Francisco, California.
It occurred in 1940.
It occurred in 1941.
It occurred in 1942.
It occurred in 1961.
It occurred in 1995.
It occurred in 1999.
It occurred in 2012.
It occurred from June 2021 to 2024.
It occurred as of January 2026.
HeldIn: It was held in Treasure Island's Golden Gate International Exposition.
It was held in the Art in Action exhibition.
It was held in the Roberts Family Gallery at the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art.
About: It is about the marriage of the artistic expression of the North and of the South on this Continent.
It is about the blending of the art of the Indian, the Mexican, the Eskimo, with the urge that makes the machine.
It is about American art being the result of a conjunction between the creative mechanism of the North and the creative power of the South.
It is about the inspiration artists in North America should take from New World native arts.
It is about the multicultural artistic expression forming into a unified cultural entity.
It is about the eventual unity of the Americas.
It is about the creative genius of the South growing from religious fervor and a native talent for plastic expression.
It is about elements from past and present.
It is about the plastification of creative power of the Northern Mechanism by union with the plastic tradition of the South.
It is about trends of creative effort in the United States and the rise of woman in various fields of creative endeavor through her use of the power of manmade machinery.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Art in Action exhibition.
It was influenced by the Golden Gate International Exposition.
It was influenced by the destruction of Man at the Crossroads.
It was influenced by Europe plunging back into war.
It was influenced by Roland J. McKinney's statement.
It was influenced by traditional deep-rooted Southern Indian forms.
It was influenced by New World native arts.
It was influenced by Mexican artistry.
It was influenced by US technology.
It was influenced by Rivera's firmly held belief that multicultural artistic expression will form into a unified cultural entity.
Influenced: It influenced the design of the CCSF campus theater lobby.
It influenced the construction of a future Diego Rivera Performing Arts Center.
